name: Vosk
description: Offline open-source speech-recognition toolkit with small models

developer.comment: Maintained by Alpha Cephei Inc.; vosk-api is the open-source repo.

first-release.comment: vosk-api GitHub repo created 2019-09-03; earliest tagged release available via the API is 0.3.3 (Feb 2020). Using 2019 as first public availability; exact month uncertain.

status.comment: Repo actively maintained; last push June 2026 (checked 2026-06-12).

primary-type.comment: An open-source speech-recognition toolkit/library a technical user runs themselves; used for transcription and command/dictation pipelines.

real-time-dictation.comment: Vosk is a recognition library, not an end-user speak-to-type app; it provides a streaming API but no system-wide dictation input method out of the box.

file-transcription.comment: Can transcribe audio files (e.g. WAV) via its API; commonly used for subtitling lectures/interviews.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting-bot or built-in call/system-audio capture; only processes audio fed to it.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Provides a streaming API with zero-latency / continuous large-vocabulary transcription.

audio-sources.comment: Accepts microphone/streaming audio and audio files programmatically. No meeting-bot or built-in system-audio capture.

underlying-engine.comment: Vosk's own engine, built on the Kaldi speech-recognition toolkit with its own acoustic/language models.

language-count.comment: Vendor states 20+ languages and dialects; individual models cover 30+ languages/dialects across the model list. Approx.

english-accuracy.comment: Accuracy varies strongly by model: small (~50MB) models are usable but error-prone; large server models are competitive. Generally below Whisper-class models in independent comparisons. No single authoritative independent WER for 'Vosk' as a whole; rating reflects the mixed/medium tier.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Each model is language-specific; the user selects the model. No automatic language detection across a single multilingual model.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Supports reconfigurable vocabulary / grammar (restrict recognition to a word list) and model adaptation for domain terms.

speaker-diarization.comment: Offers speaker identification (embeddings via a separate speaker model) but not automatic who-said-what diarization producing labeled-speaker transcript segments out of the box.

timestamps.comment: Word-level results include start/end times and confidence in the JSON output (word times optional via API).

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Pure recognition toolkit; no summaries or notes generation.

translation.comment: Speech-to-text only; no transcript translation feature.

export-formats.comment: Native output is JSON (words with confidence and timestamps) plus plain text; other formats (srt/docx/etc.) are left to the calling application.

processing-location.comment: Runs fully locally; works offline even on lightweight devices.

data-used-for-training.comment: Self-hosted and offline; audio never leaves the device, so it cannot be collected by the vendor for training.

data-deletion-control.comment: All data is local; the user fully controls storage and deletion.

platforms.comment: Runs on servers/desktops (Linux, Windows, macOS), Android, iOS, and Raspberry Pi. No standalone web app (it is a library).

system-wide-dictation.comment: No built-in OS-wide text-injection dictation; that would have to be built on top of the library.

integrations.comment: A library, not a meeting/app integration product; no Zoom/Meet/Teams/CRM integrations.

api-automation.comment: It IS an API/library, with bindings for Python, Java, Node.js, C#, C++, Rust, Go and others.

pricing-model.comment: Apache-2.0 open source; toolkit and models are free to download and use.

free-tier.comment: Fully free and open source; unlimited local use.

free-allowance.comment: No usage cap; only practical cost is local compute/setup effort.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Open-source toolkit with no subscription; free to run locally.
